assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md: 'Classification: CODE CHANGE REQUEST' and 'Sub-task creation is triggered for this comment.' subtask-30001.md exists with full sub-task description. report.md Review Feedback Processing table shows '30001 | reviewer-a | ... | CODE CHANGE REQUEST | Sub-task created'.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md: 'Classification: SUGGESTION'. Reasoning section explicitly discusses 'should also' as 'additive guidance ("also"), not a correction of an existing defect' and 'would help' as 'explicitly suggestive language indicating a potential improvement, not a required change'. Also discusses 'Something like' as 'proposes an approach without insisting on it'.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md: 'Classification: NIT' and 'No sub-task created. Nit-level feedback does not trigger sub-task creation.' No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md: 'Classification: QUESTION' and 'No sub-task created. Questions request clarification and do not trigger sub-task creation.' No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 3), '## Description' (line 9), '## Files to Modify' (line 12), '## Implementation Notes' (line 15), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 21). Only one sub-task was created (for comment 30001).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md line 31: '## Target PR' followed by line 32: 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744'. The URL matches exactly.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md lines 34-38: '## Review Context' section includes 'Comment ID: 30001', 'Author: reviewer-a', 'File: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs, line 60', and the full original comment text quoted verbatim about wrapping operations in self.db.transaction.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 output files are report/analysis files: report.md (verification report), review-30001.md through review-30004.md (review classifications), subtask-30001.md (sub-task description). No .rs, .toml, .sql, or other code files exist in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 review comments have corresponding classification files: review-30001.md (CODE CHANGE REQUEST), review-30002.md (SUGGESTION), review-30003.md (NIT), review-30004.md (QUESTION). The report's Review Feedback Processing table lists all 4 comments with their classifications and actions.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md final line: 'This report was generated by the verify-pr skill. It does NOT auto-merge the PR. A human reviewer decides whether to merge.' The overall result is WARN with no merge action taken.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only new test files added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file with 5 test functions)'. Detailed section confirms: 'The only test file in the PR diff is tests/api/sbom_delete.rs, which is a new file (not present on the base branch). New test files are inherently additive per constraint 1.20.'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a full 'Convention Upgrade Eligibility Evaluation' section checking: (1) CONVENTIONS.md - 'does not document any convention about index creation', (2) Codebase patterns - 'no codebase pattern count can be established', (3) Task specification - 'does not mention index creation'. report.md Style/Conventions section also evaluates: 'CONVENTIONS.md check... Codebase pattern check... Conclusion: No documented convention or demonstrated codebase pattern supports upgrading this suggestion.'

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md: 'No sub-task created. The suggestion remains classified as a suggestion.' No subtask-30002.md file exists. report.md Review Feedback Processing table: '30002 | reviewer-a | ... | SUGGESTION | No sub-task (no convention backing for upgrade)'. Convention upgrade evaluation concludes: 'Without documented convention evidence... there is no basis to upgrade this suggestion to a code change request.'

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Eval Quality section (lines 136-138): 'No eval result reviews exist in the PR. The 3-criteria detection (author `github-actions[bot]`, marker `## Eval Results`, footer `sdlc-workflow/run-evals`) found no matching reviews among the PR reviews. Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination.' Summary table: 'Test Quality | PASS | Eval Quality: N/A.'
